north star

Guaranteeing good output with AI systems is hard. We'll help you get better at it.

Define what good looks like. → Set criteria. → Measure against them. → Optimize.

Most teams reach for this too late, once the system is in production and the criteria have already been set implicitly by whoever built it. We help companies get ahead of that.

What we do

We work hands-on with a small number of companies to map their AI system, find where the gap between business intent and engineering measurement lives, and close it. Because we're building tooling in this space, you get direct attention from the people who care about this problem most — and direct input into what gets built.

Not a consultancy. Not a report. A working engagement with people who are building something here.

Services

  • Eval criteria audit Who defined "good"? Are those criteria actually being measured? We surface the gap and help close it.
  • Business–engineering alignment A working session to establish shared success criteria in product language that engineering can instrument against.
  • Observability review What signals are being captured and are they the right ones — connecting traces to outcomes, not just latency.
  • Evaluation pipeline design Building or improving an eval pipeline grounded in business-defined criteria, with data, tooling, and feedback loops.
  • Architecture & data flow mapping A clear diagram of the stack and where business intent drops out of the loop — often something companies don't have themselves.
  • Stack optimization Once success criteria exist, everything else — spend, retrieval quality, model choice — becomes optimizable against something real.

How an engagement works

  1. Kick-off. Goals, stack overview, access setup.
  2. Discovery. Two or three conversations going deep on each layer — evals, data, models, cost, and who owns what.
  3. Mapping. We produce a diagram of the system and locate where the gap lives.
  4. Readout. We present findings and a prioritized list of interventions, ranked by impact.
  5. Deliverable. A written report with the architecture diagram, findings, and recommendations you can act on immediately.

Why work with us

We're early, which means two things. You get our full attention — this isn't a scaled offering where you're handed to a junior. And the work directly shapes what we build. Companies we work with now will have direct input into tooling that eventually automates what we're currently doing by hand.

We're selective. We want to work with companies doing interesting things in production — not proof of concepts, not greenfield. If your AI system is live and you're not confident you'd know if it got worse, that's the conversation.

daniel@biene.club

north star — 2026
